compositions.composition_note: Equiatomic composition, 20 at.% each element; lattice constant 3.552 Å from EAM potential; minimal chemical short-range order confirmed via WC parameter analysis
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Molecular dynamics simulation using LAMMPS with Farkas and Caro EAM potential; simulation cell built with 4 nm cuboidal cells populated randomly with five elements; lattice parameter 3.552 Å determined via energy minimization; Volterra displacement fields used to insert 1/2[101̄] dislocation; slip system [101](111); periodic BC in X and Z directions, fixed boundaries in Y; energy minimization via nonlinear conjugate gradient; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat equilibration; average velocity calculated over last 300 ps of 600 ps simulation. || simplified: LAMMPS MD with EAM potential; FCC lattice parameter 3.552 Å; 1/2[101̄] dislocation via Volterra fields; slip system [101](111); 600 ps simulations with 300 ps averaging
compositions.equipment: LAMMPS MD simulator with OVITO visualization; Nosé-Hoover thermostat and barostat
compositions.xrd_details: FCC crystal structure confirmed; lattice constant 3.552 Å; intrinsic stacking fault energy 62.4 mJ/m²; correlation length analysis using DXA algorithm in OVITO
compositions.microstructure_description: original: Random FCC solid solution with local lattice distortions due to atomic size mismatch among Fe, Ni, Cr, Co, Cu; dislocation splits into Shockley partial dislocations with stacking fault; significant waviness observed in dislocation core due to variable energetic landscape; intrinsic SFE 62.4 mJ/m² on (111) plane. || simplified: FCC solid solution with partial dislocation splitting; wavy dislocation core structure due to chemical complexity; intrinsic SFE 62.4 mJ/m²
compositions.grain_size_text: N/A - atomistic simulation cell; correlation lengths 2.6-5.8 nm for screw and edge dislocations in equiatomic HEA

characterisation.XRD: FCC crystal structure confirmed via lattice parameter optimization; correlation length analysis for dislocation waviness
characterisation.Molecular_Dynamics: LAMMPS MD simulations with Farkas and Caro EAM potential; dislocation extraction algorithm (DXA) in OVITO for dislocation identification
characterisation.CSRO_Analysis: Hybrid Monte Carlo/MD method with Warren-Cowley parameter; minor CSRO observed (largest positive WC ~0.04 for Cu-Ni pair)
characterisation.Mobility_Fitting: Offset method fitting of dislocation mobility law b*tau = B*v + D*(v-v0)^alpha

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; phonon damping regime with tau increment 10 MPa
properties_of_composition.note: Drag coefficient B divided by temperature; non-linear temperature dependence observed
properties_of_composition.additional_information: B/T ratio variation 40% for screw and 36% for edge dislocations between 300-700K indicates non-linear temperature dependence in HEA vs linear in pure Ni

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 500 K; phonon damping regime
properties_of_composition.note: B/T variation 40% for screw between 300-700K
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Non-linear temperature dependence unlike pure Ni

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 700 K; phonon damping regime
properties_of_composition.note: Non-linear temperature dependence; B/T=0.969×10⁻⁷ at 700K
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Relative B/T variation 40% for screw and 36% for edge between 300-700K

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; phonon damping regime
properties_of_composition.note: Higher drag coefficient than pure Ni
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Critical shear stress ~100-110 MPa for screw dislocations (order of magnitude higher than Ni/Cu ~5 MPa)

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; full mobility law including radiative damping regime
properties_of_composition.note: Offset fitting method used; D×10⁻⁷=2.325, α=2.4, v0=1.025 nm/ps for screw
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Mobility law fitted using offset method

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; full mobility law including radiative damping regime
properties_of_composition.note: Offset fitting method used; D×10⁻⁷=10.114, α=3.1, v0=1.5815 nm/ps for edge
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Transition stress 350-400 MPa for screw dislocations

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; positive motion direction with 60° leading Shockley partial
properties_of_composition.note: 30° dislocation mobility differs from homogeneous metals
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Critical shear stress 95 MPa; steepest slope in phonon drag region; highest transition velocity

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K
properties_of_composition.note: Edge dislocations require higher stress to initiate motion in HEA
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Higher critical shear stress than screw, 30°, 60° dislocations

properties_of_composition.value: Standard deviation 0.1-0.5 range depending on applied shear stress
properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300, 500, 700 K; measured as standard deviation of partial dislocation position variation
properties_of_composition.test_specimen: Screw and edge dislocations; various character angles
properties_of_composition.note: Temperature influence on waviness increases with applied shear stress; steeper slope than pure Ni
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Waviness standard deviation measured along dislocation core; waviness decreases with increasing applied shear stress

compositions.composition_note: Ni increased to 30 at.%, other elements at 17.5 at.% each; lattice constant 3.548 Å; explores influence of chemical misfit by increasing Ni content
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Same MD methodology as equiatomic HEA; lattice parameter 3.548 Å from energy minimization; 1/2[101̄] dislocation inserted via Volterra displacement fields; 600 ps simulations. || simplified: LAMMPS MD with EAM potential; Ni-rich composition; same simulation methodology as equiatomic HEA
compositions.xrd_details: FCC structure; lattice constant 3.548 Å; intrinsic SFE 62.8 mJ/m²
compositions.microstructure_description: original: Random FCC solid solution with higher Ni content (30 at.%); dislocation behavior studied with four character angles; higher mobility than equiatomic HEA. || simplified: FCC solid solution; Ni-rich composition; higher dislocation mobility than equiatomic HEA

properties_of_composition.note: Ni-rich HEA shows highest mobility among HEAs studied
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Higher velocity than equiatomic and Cu-rich HEAs for both screw and edge dislocations

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; full mobility law fitted via offset method
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Higher mobility than equiatomic HEA due to lower chemical complexity

compositions.composition_note: Cu increased to 30 at.%, other elements at 17.5 at.% each; lattice constant 3.560 Å; Ni and Cu have smallest and largest atom sizes respectively among the five elements
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Same MD methodology as equiatomic HEA; lattice parameter 3.560 Å from energy minimization; 1/2[101̄] dislocation inserted via Volterra displacement fields; 600 ps simulations. || simplified: LAMMPS MD with EAM potential; Cu-rich composition; same simulation methodology as equiatomic HEA
compositions.xrd_details: FCC structure; lattice constant 3.560 Å; intrinsic SFE 60.4 mJ/m²
compositions.microstructure_description: original: Random FCC solid solution with higher Cu content (30 at.%); largest atom size among elements increases local distortion; dislocation mobility studied with four character angles. || simplified: FCC solid solution; Cu-rich composition; increased local lattice distortion due to larger Cu atoms

compositions.composition_note: Pure Ni reference; FCC lattice constant 3.521 Å (3.52 Å experimental); cohesive energy -4.450 eV/atom; highest atom density among elements studied; linear phonon drag coefficient dependence on temperature
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Pure Ni reference simulation using same LAMMPS methodology; lattice parameter 3.521 Å; EAM potential from Mishin et al.; 600 ps simulations with velocity averaging over 300 ps. || simplified: Pure Ni reference; LAMMPS MD; same methodology as HEA simulations
compositions.xrd_details: FCC structure; lattice constant 3.521 Å (experimental 3.52 Å); intrinsic SFE 123.5 mJ/m²
compositions.microstructure_description: original: Pure FCC Ni reference; dislocation behavior compared to HEA; linear temperature dependence of drag coefficient B consistent with Leibfried model; lower critical shear stress (~5 MPa) than HEAs (~100-110 MPa); higher dislocation velocity than HEA across stress range. || simplified: Pure FCC Ni reference; linear phonon drag; lowest critical shear stress; highest dislocation mobility

characterisation.Molecular_Dynamics: LAMMPS MD simulations with Farkas and Caro EAM potential for Ni
characterisation.Mobility_Fitting: Offset method fitting of dislocation mobility law; linear B/T dependence confirmed

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300, 500, 700 K; phonon damping regime
properties_of_composition.note: Linear temperature dependence confirmed; B/T variation 6% (300-500K) and 11% (500-700K)
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Linear B/T variation ~16% between 300-700K; consistent with Leibfried model for phonon damping

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300, 500, 700 K; phonon damping regime
properties_of_composition.note: Edge dislocations show similar linear behavior
properties_of_composition.additional_information: B/T relative difference ~16% between 300-700K; linear temperature dependence

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; full mobility law fitted via offset method
properties_of_composition.note: Reference baseline for HEA comparison; lower drag coefficient than HEAs
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Order of magnitude lower critical stress than HEAs; velocity nearly linear in tau/T up to 0.3 MPa/K

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K; full mobility law fitted via offset method
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Edge dislocations have higher mobility than screw in pure Ni (consistent with prior studies)

properties_of_composition.measurement_condition: at 300 K
properties_of_composition.note: Consistent with prior studies on homogeneous FCC metals
properties_of_composition.additional_information: Edge dislocations always move faster than screw in pure Ni

compositions.composition_note: Pure Cu reference; largest atom size among elements; FCC lattice constant 3.621 Å; cohesive energy -3.540 eV/atom; lowest intrinsic SFE (43.2 mJ/m²) among elements studied; velocity higher than HEA at low stress but lower in radiative damping regime
compositions.processing_conditions: original: Pure Cu reference simulation using same LAMMPS methodology; lattice parameter 3.621 Å; 600 ps simulations with velocity averaging over 300 ps. || simplified: Pure Cu reference; LAMMPS MD; same methodology as HEA simulations
compositions.xrd_details: FCC structure; lattice constant 3.621 Å (experimental 3.62 Å); intrinsic SFE 43.2 mJ/m²
compositions.microstructure_description: original: Pure FCC Cu reference; smallest stacking fault energy (43.2 mJ/m²) among elements studied; dislocation behavior compared to HEA; similar critical shear stress to Ni (~5 MPa); velocity higher than HEA at low stress but lower than HEA in radiative damping regime; thermal waviness similar to HEAs at high stress. || simplified: Pure FCC Cu reference; lowest SFE; moderate critical stress; velocity crossover with HEA behavior

properties_of_composition.note: Reference baseline; lowest v0 among materials
